Context.purpose: Ship v1 as a thin MCP proxy over tg-chat-rag with OAuth-only authorization for /mcp and an internal admin surface for operator login and diagnostics.
Context.scope: Expose search_messages, get_message_context, get_related_messages, and list_sources on /mcp; provide /admin/login and /admin/ops while removing API key lifecycle from the product surface.

constraints.constraint-1: External MCP callers cannot provide filters.chat_ids.
constraints.constraint-2: search_messages always uses chat IDs from TG_CHAT_RAG_CHAT_IDS env.
constraints.constraint-3: All upstream tg-chat-rag calls use Bearer token from TG_CHAT_RAG_BEARER_TOKEN.
constraints.constraint-4: /mcp accepts only OAuth 2.0 access tokens issued by Logto and validated with issuer, audience, expiry, and required scopes.
constraints.constraint-5: ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN is valid only for /admin/login and must never authorize /mcp.
constraints.constraint-6: API key CRUD and API key repository are removed from runtime architecture.
constraints.constraint-7: OAuth metadata for protected resource is published at /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource and /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/mcp.
constraints.constraint-8: OAuth client registration and refresh token lifecycle are owned by Logto; server acts only as resource server.
constraints.constraint-9: Refresh token policy target is 90 days at authorization server configuration level.
constraints.constraint-10: Auth guard is implemented using mcp-auth library (v0.2.0) with jose-backed JWT verification, replacing hand-rolled JWKS/JWT/WebCrypto code.
constraints.constraint-11: mcp-auth has express as peerDep; server uses Bun.serve() — only framework-agnostic mcp-auth internals are used (TokenVerifier, fetchServerConfig, createResourceMetadataEndpoint, BearerWWWAuthenticateHeader, error types).

contract.purpose: Load and validate runtime configuration for MCP proxy transport, admin authentication, and OAuth resource server validation settings.

export-loadConfig.PURPOSE: Validate env and return typed runtime config

type-AppConfig.PURPOSE: Typed app config with tg-chat-rag, admin auth, and OAuth resource server settings

notes.note-9: Required env: OAUTH_REQUIRED_SCOPES (CSV, default contains mcp:access)
notes.note-10: REMOVED: OAUTH_JWKS_CACHE_TTL_MS, OAUTH_JWKS_TIMEOUT_MS, OAUTH_CLOCK_SKEW_SEC — jose handles JWKS caching internally.

contract.purpose: Provide structured logs for MCP requests, OAuth validation decisions, admin actions, upstream calls, and failures.
Create and expose Drizzle PostgreSQL database client from DATABASE_URL runtime configuration for current and upcoming data modules.

export-createDb.PURPOSE: Initialize Drizzle client and pooled PostgreSQL connection

type-DbClient.PURPOSE: Typed Drizzle database handle used by repositories

contract.purpose: Define and export MCP tool schemas and tool allowlist for the proxy surface.

export-SearchMessagesInputPublic.PURPOSE: Public schema for search_messages with filters.chat_ids forbidden

export-PROXIED_TOOL_NAMES.PURPOSE: Constant allowlist for proxied tool names

contract.purpose: Call tg-chat-rag methods API via POST /api/v1/methods/<tool_name> with service-level Bearer auth.

export-callMethod.PURPOSE: Execute proxied tool call to tg-chat-rag methods API

contract.purpose: Initialize mcp-auth library with Logto OIDC discovery and expose framework-agnostic token verification and metadata utilities for Bun.serve() integration.

export-initMcpAuth.PURPOSE: Async init: fetch Logto OIDC metadata, create MCPAuth instance, extract TokenVerifier-based verify/validate functions

export-McpAuthContext.PURPOSE: Type for the initialized auth context returned by initMcpAuth

notes.note-1: Uses fetchServerConfig(OAUTH_ISSUER, { type: 'oidc' }) to get Logto metadata at startup.
notes.note-2: Creates MCPAuth in resource server mode with protectedResources config.
notes.note-3: Extracts TokenVerifier via deep import from mcp-auth/lib/auth/token-verifier.js for createVerifyJwtFunction() and getJwtIssuerValidator().
notes.note-4: Uses createResourceMetadataEndpoint(resource) to compute well-known URL.
notes.note-5: jose handles JWKS caching/rotation internally — no manual cache config needed.

contract.purpose: Authorize /mcp requests using mcp-auth token verification in Bun.serve() context and build RFC 9728-compliant challenge responses for denied requests.

export-authorizeMcpRequest.PURPOSE: Extract Bearer token from Request, call mcp-auth verify, validate issuer/audience/scopes, return allow/deny

export-createDeniedResponse.PURPOSE: Build 401/403 Response with WWW-Authenticate header using BearerWWWAuthenticateHeader from mcp-auth

export-createInitialMcpChallenge.PURPOSE: Build initial 401 with resource_metadata challenge for unauthenticated requests

notes.note-1: Token verification is delegated entirely to mcp-auth (jose-backed). No hand-rolled JWT/JWKS code.
notes.note-2: Error handling maps MCPAuthBearerAuthError and MCPAuthTokenVerificationError to appropriate HTTP responses.
notes.note-3: WWW-Authenticate header built using mcp-auth's BearerWWWAuthenticateHeader utility.

contract.purpose: Authenticate admin operators with ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN and enforce cookie session checks for /admin routes.

export-authenticateAdmin.PURPOSE: Verify login token and produce admin session cookie

export-requireAdminSession.PURPOSE: Check admin session and decide allow/redirect

export-clearAdminSession.PURPOSE: Clear admin auth cookie on logout

contract.purpose: Apply policy-safe normalization and proxy supported MCP tool calls to tg-chat-rag.

export-executeTool.PURPOSE: Validate args, inject internal chat_ids policy, call upstream, normalize result

contract.purpose: Render admin HTML with Bun JSX for operator login and OAuth runtime diagnostics.

export-renderAdminLayout.PURPOSE: Render operator layout with OAuth status tab

export-renderOpsStatus.PURPOSE: Render OAuth and MCP runtime status diagnostics

contract.purpose: Register MCP tools and route authorized tool calls into ToolProxyService.

export-registerTools.PURPOSE: Register proxied MCP tools except list_chats

M-SERVER.NAME: ServerMain

contract.purpose: Boot Bun HTTP server, initialize mcp-auth provider at startup, enforce auth guards, and expose /mcp, /admin/*, OAuth well-known routes, and /healthz.

export-main.PURPOSE: Boot sequence with async mcp-auth init, dependency wiring, and route lifecycle

M-SERVER.depends: M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-DB, M-ADMIN-AUTH, M-ADMIN-UI, M-MCP-AUTH-PROVIDER, M-MCP-AUTH-GUARD, M-TRANSPORT

notes.note-1: main() calls initMcpAuth() at startup — this fetches Logto OIDC metadata and validates config before server starts.
notes.note-2: Discovery metadata route uses MCPAuth.protectedResourceMetadataRouter() output converted to Bun handler, or manual metadata response built from MCPAuth config.

contract.purpose: Unit verification of mcp-auth-backed guard decision mapping and challenge header formatting.

export-McpAuthGuardTests.PURPOSE: Checks missing header, invalid token, insufficient scope, valid token, and error response formatting

contract.purpose: Integration verification of discovery metadata, auth challenge flow, and authorized MCP dispatch.

export-ServerIntegrationTests.PURPOSE: Validates discovery metadata, initial challenge, token-based auth, and handshake coherence

DF-ADMIN-LOGIN.NAME: Admin Login Flow
DF-ADMIN-LOGIN.step-1: Operator submits login form with ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN candidate.
DF-ADMIN-LOGIN.step-2: M-ADMIN-AUTH validates token and issues signed HTTP-only admin session cookie.

DF-ADMIN-GUARD.NAME: Admin Route Guard Flow
DF-ADMIN-GUARD.step-4: Authenticated request is routed to M-ADMIN-UI handlers.

DF-OAUTH-DISCOVERY.NAME: OAuth Protected Resource Discovery Flow
DF-OAUTH-DISCOVERY.step-1: MCP client requests protected resource metadata for /mcp.
DF-OAUTH-DISCOVERY.step-2: M-SERVER serves metadata built from MCPAuth protectedResources config (resource, authorization_servers, scopes_supported, bearer_methods_supported).
DF-OAUTH-DISCOVERY.step-3: Client starts OAuth authorization against Logto and obtains access token.

DF-MCP-AUTH.NAME: MCP OAuth Auth Flow
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-1: Client sends Authorization header with OAuth Bearer access token.
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-2: M-MCP-AUTH-GUARD extracts Bearer token from Request headers.
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-3: Guard calls mcp-auth verifyAccessToken(token) which uses jose for JWT signature verification via JWKS.
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-4: Guard validates issuer via mcp-auth validateIssuer() and checks audience + required scopes.
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-5: Unauthorized request returns 401 with WWW-Authenticate built via BearerWWWAuthenticateHeader.
DF-MCP-AUTH.step-6: Authorized request is dispatched to M-TRANSPORT with AuthInfo context.

DF-PROXY-SEARCH.NAME: search_messages Proxy Flow
DF-PROXY-SEARCH.step-1: Authorized client calls MCP tool search_messages.
DF-PROXY-SEARCH.step-2: M-TRANSPORT validates against public schema that forbids filters.chat_ids.
DF-PROXY-SEARCH.step-4: M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT sends POST {TG_CHAT_RAG_BASE_URL}/api/v1/methods/search_messages with Bearer token.
DF-PROXY-SEARCH.step-5: Response is normalized and returned to MCP client.

DF-PROXY-CONTEXT.NAME: get_message_context Proxy Flow
DF-PROXY-CONTEXT.step-1: Authorized client calls MCP tool get_message_context.
DF-PROXY-CONTEXT.step-5: Upstream response returns unchanged in MCP result envelope.

DF-PROXY-RELATED.NAME: get_related_messages Proxy Flow
DF-PROXY-RELATED.step-1: Authorized client calls MCP tool get_related_messages.
DF-PROXY-RELATED.step-5: Upstream response returns unchanged in MCP result envelope.

DF-PROXY-SOURCES.NAME: list_sources Proxy Flow
DF-PROXY-SOURCES.step-1: Authorized client calls MCP tool list_sources.
DF-PROXY-SOURCES.step-5: Upstream response returns unchanged in MCP result envelope.

DF-TOOL-EXCLUSION.NAME: list_chats Exclusion Flow
DF-TOOL-EXCLUSION.step-2: M-TRANSPORT has no registration for list_chats.
DF-TOOL-EXCLUSION.step-3: Client receives MCP tool-not-found behavior; no upstream call is performed.

Phase-1.name: Existing Proxy Foundation
Phase-1.step-1: Implemented typed env parsing and strict validation for TG_CHAT_RAG_* variables.
Phase-1.step-2: Implemented structured logger and request-scoped fields.
Phase-1.step-3: Implemented public schemas and proxied tool allowlist constants.
Phase-1.step-4: Implemented upstream tg-chat-rag adapter with timeout and normalized errors.
Phase-1.step-5: Implemented MCP transport registration for the public tool surface.

Phase-2.name: OAuth Contract Rewrite
Phase-2.step-1: Extend config contract with PUBLIC_URL and OAuth validation settings.
Phase-2.step-2: Replace API key authorization contract with OAuth-only guard contract.
Phase-2.step-3: Replace API key management scope with operator diagnostics scope.

Phase-3.name: OAuth Core Validation
Phase-3.step-1: Implement issuer JWKS fetch + cache + refresh behavior.
Phase-3.step-2: Implement JWT validation with claim and scope checks.
Phase-3.step-3: Wire validator outputs to allow/deny decisions and challenge metadata.

Phase-4.name: OAuth Protocol Surface
Phase-4.step-2: Mount discovery routes and ensure /mcp 401 responses include WWW-Authenticate.
Phase-4.step-3: Verify OAuth challenge behavior with Claude-compatible flow.

Phase-5.name: Legacy API Key Removal
Phase-5.step-1: Remove API key repository dependency from /mcp auth path and startup wiring.
Phase-5.step-2: Remove /admin/api-keys routes, forms, and fragments from operator UI.
Phase-5.step-3: Remove obsolete API key-specific config notes and align runtime docs to OAuth-only.

Phase-6.name: Cutover Validation
Phase-6.step-1: Add OAuth smoke tests: missing token, invalid token, insufficient scope, authorized token.
Phase-6.step-2: Validate end-to-end MCP OAuth handshake with Logto DCR-enabled client.
Phase-6.step-3: Validate admin login and ops routes remain stable after API key removal.

Phase-7.name: mcp-auth Library Migration
Phase-7.step-1: Remove OAUTH_JWKS_CACHE_TTL_MS, OAUTH_JWKS_TIMEOUT_MS, OAUTH_CLOCK_SKEW_SEC from config — jose handles JWKS caching internally. Keep OAUTH_ISSUER, OAUTH_AUDIENCE, OAUTH_REQUIRED_SCOPES, PUBLIC_URL. Update AppConfig type and loadConfig validation.
Phase-7.step-2: Create src/auth/mcp-auth-provider.ts: async initMcpAuth() that fetches Logto OIDC metadata via fetchServerConfig(), creates MCPAuth in resource server mode, extracts TokenVerifier via deep import, returns McpAuthContext with verifyAccessToken, validateIssuer, resourceMetadataUrl, and snakecase metadata payload.
Phase-7.step-3: Rewrite src/auth/mcp-auth-guard.ts to use McpAuthContext from provider: extract Bearer token from Request, call verifyAccessToken(token) → AuthInfo, validate issuer/audience/scopes via mcp-auth errors, build WWW-Authenticate via BearerWWWAuthenticateHeader, return allow/deny decision with AuthInfo or Response.
Phase-7.step-4: Update src/server/index.ts: call initMcpAuth() at startup, replace hand-rolled oauthTokenValidator/mcpAuthDeps wiring with McpAuthContext, replace handleOAuthProtectedResourceMetadata() with metadata served from McpAuthContext, update /mcp route to use new guard API.
Phase-7.step-5: Delete src/auth/oauth-jwks-client.ts, src/auth/oauth-token-validator.ts, src/auth/oauth-discovery-routes.ts — all replaced by mcp-auth library.
Phase-7.step-6: Rewrite auth guard tests for new mcp-auth-backed guard: mock McpAuthContext, test missing header, invalid token, insufficient scope, valid token, error response formatting.
Phase-7.step-7: Rewrite server integration tests: discovery metadata coherence, initial challenge format, token-based auth flow. Delete old oauth-jwks-client.test.ts, oauth-token-validator.test.ts, oauth-discovery-routes.test.ts.
Phase-7.step-8: Update config tests for removed JWKS/clock-skew env vars.

RISK-1.name: Express Peer Dependency Incompatibility
RISK-1.impact: mcp-auth declares express@^5 as peerDep. Bun.serve() does not use Express. MCPAuth.protectedResourceMetadataRouter() and bearerAuth() return Express middleware.
RISK-1.mitigation: Only use framework-agnostic internals: fetchServerConfig, TokenVerifier (deep import), createResourceMetadataEndpoint, BearerWWWAuthenticateHeader, error types. Never import Express routers/middleware. If express peerDep warning is noisy, add to package.json peerDependenciesMeta.

RISK-2.name: Deep Import Stability
RISK-2.impact: Importing TokenVerifier from mcp-auth/lib/auth/token-verifier.js uses internal paths that may change between mcp-auth versions.
RISK-2.mitigation: Pin mcp-auth to 0.2.x. Document deep imports in module contract. Test import resolution in CI. If mcp-auth adds public framework-agnostic exports, migrate to those.

RISK-3.name: jose JWKS Cache Behavior Differences
RISK-3.impact: Moving from hand-rolled JWKS cache (explicit TTL) to jose's createRemoteJWKSet may behave differently under key rotation.
RISK-3.mitigation: jose's JWKS cache is battle-tested and used by millions of deployments. Its cooldownDuration and cacheMaxAge parameters can be tuned via remoteJwkSet options in VerifyJwtConfig if needed.

RISK-4.name: Startup Latency from OIDC Discovery Fetch
RISK-4.impact: initMcpAuth() fetches Logto OIDC metadata at startup. Network failure blocks server boot.
RISK-4.mitigation: Fail fast with clear error message. Consider on-demand discovery fallback ({ issuer, type: 'oidc' }) if startup fetch is unacceptable — metadata will be fetched on first request instead.

RISK-5.name: Clock Skew Tolerance Change
RISK-5.impact: Hand-rolled validator had configurable OAUTH_CLOCK_SKEW_SEC (default 60s). jose uses its own defaults.
RISK-5.mitigation: jose.jwtVerify supports clockTolerance option via JWTVerifyOptions. Pass explicit value if needed.

RISK-6.name: AuthInfo Shape Compatibility
RISK-6.impact: mcp-auth's AuthInfo extends @modelcontextprotocol/sdk AuthInfo with token, issuer, clientId, scopes, subject, audience, claims fields. Current code uses custom McpAuthDecision type.
RISK-6.mitigation: Map mcp-auth's AuthInfo to existing McpAuthDecision interface or adopt AuthInfo directly. Ensure transport handlers receive compatible auth context.
